candidate: sm120 transpose-free K-GEMM conv router (no cuDNN/nchwToNhwc); fused epilogue reused from sm75
mechanism: causal conv = K shifted (B,S,H)@(H,E) matmuls, x in native layout; kills copy_ 48% + ~870us transposes
correctness: by-construction conv-as-GEMM; bench grad parity is the gate

why: launch+copy explosion: 161 launches/iter, 240 aten::mm (42% CUDA), 760 aten::copy_ (31%, from sliced-noncontig matmul inputs + per-k .to() casts + .float()), 180 add_; total 7.42ms CUDA vs cudnn 5.57 vs compiled 4.84
lesson: multi-torch-op conv decomposition is launch/copy-bound on Blackwell too (matches T4 cuBLAS-K-GEMM 0.35x). Only a SINGLE fused Triton kernel can win.
next: candidate #2 = fused Triton conv (one launch, read-once, in-register epilogue)

candidate: single-launch fused Triton conv router (FusedConvRouterFused): fwd conv+sigmoid+topk+gather in one kernel; grad_x fused kernel (H-tiled); grad_W fused reduction kernel
mechanism: ~4 launches vs cuDNN 75, x read once, zero layout transposes/copies, bf16 tl.dot fp32-accum
note: 3 blind hand-written kernels; first run = compile+correctness(idx/grad/count)+perf gate; expect possible correctness iteration
